torch.name: a torch
torch.description: A pitch-soaked length of wood. It burns bright but not forever.

lantern.name: a brass lantern
lantern.description: A shuttered brass lantern; steadier and longer-lived than a torch.

glimmersteel-lamp.name: a glimmersteel lamp
glimmersteel-lamp.description: A lamp cased in glimmer-alloyed steel, a thread of captive light wound through the housing so the wick has only to carry the burn, not be it. It throws a clean, cold glare brighter than any brass lantern, and sips its oil so slowly a single flask outlasts three. Dear work, and prized by delvers who mean to stay down a long while.

short-sword.name: a short sword
short-sword.description: A plain, well-balanced short sword. Reliable steel.

rusted-blade.name: a rusted blade
rusted-blade.description: Pitted iron, but it still holds an edge.

prospectors-hatchet.name: a prospector's hatchet
prospectors-hatchet.description: A heavy, short-hafted axe meant for splitting kindling and prising ore from stone, pressed into harder service. It is slow to swing, but a strong arm makes it bite deep.

leather-jerkin.name: a leather jerkin
leather-jerkin.description: Stiff, abyss-cured hide. Modest protection against tooth and claw.

iron-cuirass.name: an iron cuirass
iron-cuirass.description: A heavy front-and-back plate of beaten abyssal iron, strapped over the shoulders. It turns a blade far better than hide — but the weight rides hard on a delver, and its muffling shell dulls the wariness that keeps one alive down here.

leather-helm.name: a leather helm
leather-helm.description: A boiled-leather cap reinforced with iron studs. Won't stop a true blow, but it has turned many a glancing one.

iron-helm.name: an iron helm
iron-helm.description: A plain dome of beaten iron with a narrow face-bar, dented from older heads than yours. It shrugs off blows a leather cap never could — but it sits heavy and close, and the muffled hush inside dulls a delver's ear for the dark.

umbral-mind-charm.name: an Umbral mind-charm
umbral-mind-charm.description: A flat disc of dark, river-smoothed stone hung on a cord of woven gut, its face inlaid with a single thread of glimmer worked into the lightless-eye glyph. It rests cool against the breastbone, and the longer it is worn the clearer the wearer's thoughts seem to run — as though the cold light lent the mind an edge. Umbral work, and patient: they carve these for their own glimmer-singers and part with them rarely.

apprentice-glimmer-charm.name: an apprentice's glimmer-charm
apprentice-glimmer-charm.description: A flat disc of river-smoothed stone on a frayed gut cord, its face scored with the lightless-eye glyph the Umbrals work into their charms — but the cut is a learner's, uneven and started over twice, and the thread of glimmer set into it runs thin and stops short, the work never finished. Even half-made it does what such a charm should: it sharpens the wearer's notice in the dark and lays a faint cold ward against hostile light. Mallki's teaching is plain in every careful, clumsy line of it — the first charm of a hand that never got to make a second.

umbral-glimmer-ring.name: an Umbral glimmer-ring
umbral-glimmer-ring.description: A plain band of cold grey metal, its inner face grooved with a hair-fine channel of glimmer that never quite stops glowing. Worn against the skin it deepens the well a caster draws from — a small, steady reservoir of borrowed light to spend on the weave. Old Umbral craft, the channel-work finer than any surface smith could cut.

glimmersteel-coil.name: a glimmersteel coil
glimmersteel-coil.description: A band for the finger wound from a single hair-fine wire of glimmersteel, coiled tight around a mote of pressed glimmer dust at its heart. The captive light never quite stops welling up, and worn against the skin it seeps slowly back into the wearer — so a caster's well refills even on the move, where idle hands would only crawl. Tobin's work, and the one contraption of his that does just what he claims.

glimmer-singer-circlet.name: a glimmer-singer's circlet
glimmer-singer-circlet.description: A thin band of cold grey Umbral metal for the brow, its channel inlaid with a hair-fine thread of glimmer that never quite stops glowing. It is a glimmer-singer's mark — worn by the few Umbrals taught to draw the deep art up out of the shards, and by the apprentices set on that long road. It deepens the well a caster draws from and turns a little hostile magic aside, yet sits light as a whisper, no helm at all. This one is tarnished and cold, its light gone faint, as though it had been worn a long time in the dark by someone who had forgotten what it was for.

ring-of-sight.name: a ring of sight
ring-of-sight.description: A slim silver band graven with a fine, open eye. The pattern is an old silversmith's conceit, but worked to Vesper's method it does more than flatter — it sharpens the wearer's notice, the flick of movement at the dark's edge and the seam a careless delver walks straight past.

ring-of-wits.name: a ring of wits
ring-of-wits.description: A slim silver band chased with a knot of interlacing lines that draws the eye in and never quite lets it settle. Worked to Vesper's method, it keeps the wearer's thoughts quick and their guard quicker — the small, steady edge that turns a blow aside or shrugs off a hostile weave.

warding-ring.name: a warding ring
warding-ring.description: A slim band of tarnished silver, its face graven with a close ring of overlapping scales. Worked to Vesper's method, the pattern does more than flatter — it sits between the wearer and a hostile weave, turning a little of the magic aside before it bites. Whoever last wore it came down into the dark and never climbed out; it lay among the husks and picked bones of the nest until a luckier hand thought to look.

shards.name: shards
shards.description: Motes of crystallised light scattered on the stone — the Rim's coin. Gather them and they tally to your purse.

crystal.name: a glimmer crystal
crystal.description: A fist-sized shard of the deep's glimmer, its facets holding a slow, captive glow. Shattered, it would yield a fortune in shards — but whole, a trader will pay well for it, and a steady hand can grind it to dust.

luminescent-gland.name: a luminescent gland
luminescent-gland.description: Harvested from a lightbug; it glows faintly and cold in the palm.

vial.name: an empty vial
vial.description: A small glass vial, stoppered with wax.

lamp-oil.name: a flask of oil
lamp-oil.description: A stoppered clay flask of thick, slow-burning oil — fuel to refill a lantern.

bug-tallow.name: a lump of bug-tallow
bug-tallow.description: A fistful of pale, waxy fat rendered from under a stonebug's plates. It keeps for weeks and burns clean — the Rim's cheapest source of lamp-oil, and good frying fat for the broth-pots besides.

thornbug-egg.name: a thornbug egg
thornbug-egg.description: A single leathery egg, warm and faintly translucent, lifted whole from a clutch in the grazing range. A dark, curled shape shifts inside it when you turn it in the light. Kept warm in the hand it quickens fast — and what hatches imprints on the first thing it smells.

consumable.flavour: It uncurls, finds your scent, and trundles close at your heel.

delver-claim-tag.name: a delver's claim-tag
delver-claim-tag.description: A thumb-length tag of stamped lead on a rotted cord, the kind every registered delver wears below — claim number on one face, the holder's mark on the other. This one reads WENDER, M., the stamp still crisp under a crust of lake-silt. Its owner has no further use for it; Fenn's ledger does.

scroll-spark.name: a Scroll of Spark
scroll-spark.description: Crackling glyphs run the length of the brittle vellum, smelling faintly of a coming storm. Study it to learn the Spark cantrip — the scroll crumbles to ash in the learning.

scroll-regeneration.name: a Scroll of Regeneration
scroll-regeneration.description: Vellum dense with looping, vinework glyphs that seem to breathe faintly green in the dark. Study it to learn the Regeneration weave — a mending light cast on yourself or an ally — and the scroll crumbles to ash in the learning.

scroll-glimmerskin.name: a Scroll of Glimmerskin
scroll-glimmerskin.description: Stiff vellum edged in flaking glimmer-leaf, the glyphs raised like a rime of frost you can feel under a thumb. Study it to learn the Glimmerskin weave — a glimmer shell that hardens the skin against blade and spell — and the scroll crumbles to ash in the learning.

scroll-candlelight.name: a Scroll of Candlelight
scroll-candlelight.description: A scrap of cheap vellum, the glyphs scrawled in a hurried hand and smelling of tallow. Study it to learn the Candlelight cantrip — a mote of light to push the dark back a step — and the scroll crumbles to ash in the learning.

scroll-mage-armour.name: a Scroll of Mage Armour
scroll-mage-armour.description: Vellum ruled with a tight, geometric lattice of glyphs that catch the light like fine mail. Study it to learn the Mage Armour weave — a barding of hardened light that turns a blade — and the scroll crumbles to ash in the learning.

scroll-sleep.name: a Scroll of Sleep
scroll-sleep.description: Soft grey vellum, the glyphs looping and unhurried, heavy to look at as though the page itself were drowsy. Study it to learn the Sleep weave — a hush that folds a foe into slumber — and the scroll crumbles to ash in the learning.

scroll-bolt.name: a Scroll of Bolt
scroll-bolt.description: Heavy vellum, the glyphs gouged deep and edged in scorch, as though the spell strained the page that held it. Study it to learn the Bolt war-spell — a hurled lance of compressed light — and the scroll crumbles to ash in the learning.

scroll-glimmer-spike.name: a Scroll of Glimmer Spike
scroll-glimmer-spike.description: Stiff vellum flecked with ground glimmer that glints along the strokes of each savage glyph. Study it to learn the Glimmer Spike weave — a spike of raw glimmer that burns shards as it strikes — and the scroll crumbles to ash in the learning.

scroll-witchfire.name: a Scroll of Witchfire
scroll-witchfire.description: Greasy vellum the colour of old smoke, the glyphs charred at their edges as though the spell had tried to eat its way off the page. Study it to learn the Witchfire weave — a clinging glimmer-burn that gnaws at a foe over time — and the scroll crumbles to ash in the learning.

scroll-arc-flash.name: a Scroll of Arc Flash
scroll-arc-flash.description: Stiff vellum that seems to hold a faint charge of its own, the central glyph a tight crackle-knot ringed with warding marks; a thumb drawn across it prickles. Study it to learn the Arc Flash war-spell — a blinding crack of light that lashes every foe in the room — and the scroll crumbles to ash in the learning.

scroll-glimmer-storm.name: a Scroll of Glimmer Storm
scroll-glimmer-storm.description: Heavy vellum gone stiff and silver-flecked, the glyphs raised in ridges of ground glimmer that catch the light like a scatter of broken glass. Study it to learn the Glimmer Storm weave — a squall of raw glimmer that scores every foe in the room and burns shards as it strikes — and the scroll crumbles to ash in the learning.

scroll-glimmer-husk.name: a Scroll of Glimmer Husk
scroll-glimmer-husk.description: Cured gut-skin rather than vellum, marked in Mallki's careful hand — the binding of glimmer through a worked chitin plate until the shell wakes and stands. Study it to learn the Glimmer Husk weave — a guard raised from a plate and a fistful of glimmer — and the skin curls to nothing as you take in the last glyph.

palecap-mushroom.name: a palecap mushroom
palecap-mushroom.description: A plump, ivory-capped mushroom on a stout stem. Mild and earthy — the kind delvers learn to cook before they learn to fight.

witchglow-cap.name: a witchglow cap
witchglow-cap.description: A slender mushroom that drips a cold green light from its gills. Beautiful, and poison to eat — but the glow can be coaxed into a potion by a steady hand.

hearty-broth.name: a bowl of hearty broth
hearty-broth.description: A steaming bowl of Maeve's own broth, thick with palecap mushrooms and the dark, tender meat the Lantern's Rest breeds down in its cellar. The signature fare of the inn — it mends body and mind alike, and has sent many a shaken delver back down the shaft with their nerve restored.

minor-light-potion.name: a minor light potion
minor-light-potion.description: A small vial of cold, captured luminescence, thumbed from a lightbug's gland. Drink it and your own skin glows softly for a time.

light-potion.name: a light potion
light-potion.description: A vial of suspended luminescence, brighter and longer-lived than the minor draught. Drink it and you blaze like a held torch.

regeneration-draught.name: a regeneration draught
regeneration-draught.description: A cloudy tincture that holds a faint, cold green light deep in the glass, the witchglow's poison tamed and turned to mending. Drunk down, it does not close a wound at a stroke — it settles warm beneath the skin and knits torn flesh slowly, a little with every breath, until the warmth fades.

consumable.flavour: You drink the draught down. It sits warm and heavy in your gut, and a slow, crawling warmth begins to knit you back together.

mana-tonic.name: a mana tonic
mana-tonic.description: A vial of moss-steeped water that has kept the chasm-river's cold, and a little of its quiet. It glows the faintest blue-white, like deep water remembering light. Drunk down, it settles the mind and lets the will run clear again.

consumable.flavour: You drink the tonic down. It is cold and still and tastes of stone, and the noise behind your eyes quiets — the well of your will brimming again.

acid-bomb.name: an acid bomb
acid-bomb.description: A thin-walled vial of venom rendered down past poison into something that simply eats — amber, fuming, and faintly warm through the glass. Thrown, it bursts and the fumes cling, corroding everything in the room for a while after. One throw and it's spent.

consumable.flavour: The bomb shatters and a fume of amber acid sprays across the room, hissing and clinging where it lands.

iron-ore.name: a chunk of iron ore
iron-ore.description: A heavy, rust-streaked lump of raw iron, still wearing its skin of stone. Worthless until a forge has had its way with it.

iron-bar.name: an iron bar
iron-bar.description: A smelted ingot of dull grey iron, dense and cool in the hand. The smith's raw stock — the start of a blade, not the end of one.

iron-dagger.name: an iron dagger
iron-dagger.description: A short, plain blade forged from abyssal iron. Light and quick in the hand, if unlovely — a thing a delver can make for themselves.

rusty-hammer.name: a rusty hammer
rusty-hammer.description: A light cross-pein hammer gone orange with rust, its haft swollen and split, the head pitted and loose on the helve. Under the corrosion, stamped deep into the cheek, a maker's mark is still just legible — the same mark the Rim smith strikes into his work. Someone carried this down into the dark a long time ago, and did not carry it back.

tobins-hammer.name: Tobin's hammer
tobins-hammer.description: Tobin's first hammer, made whole again by the hand that learned its trade on it — the rust ground back to bright steel, the head re-set true and the haft bound new in oiled leather. It is light and wakes quick in the hand, balanced for a fast, snapping blow, and where the head bites clean it bites deep. The old maker's mark gleams again on the cheek. He gave it back without a word; some debts the deep doesn't get to keep.

glimmer-dust.name: a measure of glimmer dust
glimmer-dust.description: Glimmer ground to a fine, cold-glinting powder — crystal and coin alike end here. The smith's true reagent, and the alchemist's.

glimmersteel-bar.name: a glimmersteel bar
glimmersteel-bar.description: Iron alloyed with glimmer, its grey shot through with threads of cold, captive light. Harder than honest steel, and dearer — stock for a finer class of gear.

glimmersteel-sword.name: a glimmersteel sword
glimmersteel-sword.description: A long blade forged from glimmer-alloyed steel, its edge holding a faint, cold inner light that never quite fades. Lighter and meaner than honest iron, and balanced so finely it seems to lend strength to the arm that swings it. A small fortune in bars, and rarer still in the skill to work them.

glimmersteel-warhammer.name: a glimmersteel warhammer
glimmersteel-warhammer.description: A great two-fisted head of glimmer-alloyed steel on a banded haft, a vein of cold captive light pooling in the striking face. It is slow off the mark and asks a strong back — but a delver who puts their weight behind it brings the whole brutal mass down on a single point, crushing through shell and plate that would turn a blade. Tobin's heaviest work.

glimmersteel-staff.name: a glimmersteel staff
glimmersteel-staff.description: A slender rod of glimmer-alloyed steel, cool to the grip, a vein of captive light running its length and pooling bright at the head. It is no great weapon — but a focus: held while casting it steadies the will and deepens the well a caster draws from. Arcane work, half-forged and half-attuned.

glimmersteel-cuirass.name: a glimmersteel cuirass
glimmersteel-cuirass.description: A cuirass of glimmer-alloyed steel, the plate pared thin and curved close, threads of captive light running cold through the grey. It turns a blow as well as a slab of stacked chitin at a fraction of the heft — and where honest iron rides heavy and closes a delver off from the dark, the glimmer in this keeps the senses clear. Tobin's finest work, and priced like it.

glimmersteel-helm.name: a glimmersteel helm
glimmersteel-helm.description: A close dome of glimmer-alloyed steel with a narrow light-bar at the brow, a vein of cold captive light tracing the crown. It shrugs off a blow as iron does, but sits light and never muffles the ear — the glimmer keeps the wearer's wariness sharp where a plain helm would dull it.

starsilver-bar.name: a starsilver bar
starsilver-bar.description: Silver smelted through with glimmer dust until the metal holds a cold, captive shimmer all its own — frozen starlight, the old tales would have it, though no smith alive can say what glimmer truly is. Dearer than glimmersteel and finer still; stock for the rarest gear the deep allows.

searing-flare.name: a searing flare
searing-flare.description: A waxed paper cartridge packed with glimmer dust and a lightbug's gland. Cracked alight, it floods the dark with a searing glare — blinding to wield by, and agony to anything that fears the light.

consumable.flavour: It erupts in a searing white glare — the dark flees the room and light-shy things recoil and shriek.

shard-grenade.name: a shard grenade
shard-grenade.description: A fist-sized iron casing packed tight with pressed glimmer dust and a fistful of jagged shards. Wind the friction-cap, throw, and the captive light lets go all at once — a single white crack that flings razor splinters through everything in the room. One throw and it's spent; Tobin swears the trick is winding the cap, not the throwing.

consumable.flavour: The captive light lets go in a single white crack, flinging razor shards through everything in the room.

schematic-shard-grenade.name: a shard grenade schematic
schematic-shard-grenade.description: One of Tobin's soot-smudged diagrams — the packing of pressed glimmer dust against loose shards in an iron casing, and the friction-cap that sets it off on a throw. A warning is scrawled twice in the margin. Study it to fix the method in memory.

schematic-searing-flare.name: a flare schematic
schematic-searing-flare.description: One of Tobin's soot-smudged diagrams — this one actually legible: how to pack glimmer dust and a lightbug's gland into a flare that burns searing-bright. Study it to fix the method in memory.

schematic-render-oil.name: an oil-rendering method
schematic-render-oil.description: A single greasy card in Garrick's blunt hand — the rendering of stonebug tallow down over a low flame into clean-burning lamp-oil. He'll part with the method, he says, since a delver who renders their own still buys the flasks off him when the herd runs thin. Study it to fix the method in memory.

schematic-glimmersteel-coil.name: a glimmersteel coil schematic
schematic-glimmersteel-coil.description: One of Tobin's diagrams, and a proud one — the winding of a single glimmersteel wire around a mote of pressed glimmer dust so the captive light seeps back into the wearer rather than burning off. He'll tell you twice it's his own design. Study it to fix the method in memory.

schematic-glimmersteel-staff.name: a glimmersteel staff schematic
schematic-glimmersteel-staff.description: A page of Vesper's precise hand — the working and arcane attuning of glimmersteel into a caster's focus, the channel-lines that let it hold and lend back the will. Study it to fix the method in memory.

schematic-glimmersteel-lamp.name: a glimmersteel lamp schematic
schematic-glimmersteel-lamp.description: One of Tobin's diagrams, and a rare clean one — the casing of a lantern in glimmer-alloyed steel and the threading of captive light through the housing, so it burns brighter and sups its oil at a crawl. Study it to fix the method in memory.

schematic-glimmersteel-bar.name: a glimmersteel-bar schematic
schematic-glimmersteel-bar.description: The first page of Tobin's glimmer-work — the smelting of glimmer dust into molten iron so the light beds into the metal instead of guttering out, drawn off as a bar of cold-shining glimmersteel. Every blade and casing he sells begins here. Study it to fix the method in memory.

schematic-glimmer-dust.name: a glimmer-dust schematic
schematic-glimmer-dust.description: A page of Vesper's careful hand — the grinding and settling of a raw crystal into a measure of glimmer dust, the captive light loosed but not lost, ready to alloy or burn. The first thing she teaches anyone serious about the glimmer. Study it to fix the method in memory.

schematic-pressed-glimmer-dust.name: a pressed-glimmer-dust schematic
schematic-pressed-glimmer-dust.description: A note in Vesper's hand for the days the crystals run short — pressing a measure of glimmer dust from bought stock and a fistful of shards rather than grinding it from a crystal at all. Dearer, she'll grant, but it asks no crystal. Study it to fix the method in memory.

schematic-starsilver.name: a starsilver schematic
schematic-starsilver.description: A page of Vesper's careful hand — the trick of folding glimmer dust into molten silver so the light takes and holds rather than burning off in the heat. She'll tell you the 'frozen starlight' is only a story; the method, she'll grant, is real enough. Study it to fix the alloy in memory.

schematic-ring-of-sight.name: a sight-ring schematic
schematic-ring-of-sight.description: A page of Vesper's careful hand — the graving and quiet working of a silver band that sharpens its wearer's notice. Study it to fix the method in memory.

schematic-ring-of-wits.name: a wits-ring schematic
schematic-ring-of-wits.description: A page of Vesper's careful hand — the chasing and quiet working of a silver band that keeps its wearer's thoughts and guard quick. Study it to fix the method in memory.

schematic-regeneration-draught.name: a regeneration draught recipe
schematic-regeneration-draught.description: A water-stained page in Vesper's careful hand — the steeping of palecap, the patient drawing-off of the witchglow's poison, the long cold distillation that turns the two to a slow mending. Study it to fix the method in memory.

schematic-mana-tonic.name: a mana tonic recipe
schematic-mana-tonic.description: A page in Vesper's careful hand — the steeping of chasm-moss in cold still water and the drawing-off of its quiet, so a draught of it clears the mind and refills the will. Study it to fix the method in memory.

schematic-acid-bomb.name: an acid bomb recipe
schematic-acid-bomb.description: A page in Vesper's careful hand, the margins eaten through in two places — the rendering of centipede venom down past poison into a caustic that keeps eating where it lands. She notes, drily, to mix it at arm's length. Study it to fix the method in memory.

book-of-cooking.name: a book of cooking
book-of-cooking.description: A grease-spotted, hard-used kitchen book bound in cracked leather, its pages thick with Maeve's looping hand and the steam-warped recipes of the Lantern's Rest. Study it to learn the camp cook's staples all at once — the binding falls to crumbs as you take in the last page.

book-of-hearty-cooking.name: a book of hearty cooking
book-of-hearty-cooking.description: A slim, butter-stained folio in Maeve's hand, kept back from the common cookbook — the one recipe she does not give away with the rest. Pages of close notes on the long, slow building of a proper deep stew, the browning and the simmering and the patience it wants. Study it to fix the method in memory; the folio falls to crumbs as you take in the last page.

chitin-plate.name: a chitin plate
chitin-plate.description: A broad scute of grey shell prised from a stonebug, dense and faintly ridged. Worked right, it turns a blade as well as boiled leather.

dense-chitin-plate.name: a slab of dense chitin
dense-chitin-plate.description: A scute prised from one of the deep's oldest armoured things — not grey like ordinary stonebug plate, but dark-banded and near-black where mineral has worked into the shell over decades. It is heavier than it looks, notched a little by whatever took it off the beast, and rings like ceramic when you knock it.

kingshell-plate.name: a kingshell plate
kingshell-plate.description: A single great scute prised from the Pale King's back — pale, translucent shell gone yellow and stone-hard with age, and threaded through with veins of raw glimmer grown into the plate where the cold lake water lapped longest. It is heavier and richer than any stonebug's, the captive light in it lending the shell a faint, slow glow. There is only ever one, and an armourer who knew the working of it could make something well out of the ordinary.

chitin-spike.name: a chitin spike
chitin-spike.description: A hand-length barb snapped from a thornbug, needle-sharp and still wet at the root. The sort of thing a smith sets into a club, or a fool sets into their palm.

silver-ore.name: a chunk of silver ore
silver-ore.description: A heavy, pale grey lump flecked with the faint lustre of raw silver. Rarer than iron down here, and finer — the seams run thin and deep, and the rock gives it up grudgingly.

silver-bar.name: a silver bar
silver-bar.description: A pale ingot cast from smelted ore, cool and solid in the hand, bearing the faint marks of the mould. The smith's stock for fine work — harder-won than iron, and worth the getting.

slug-slime.name: a glob of slug-slime
slug-slime.description: A cold, stringy smear scraped from a scour-slug, clear as spit and twice as sticky. An alchemist's binder — a little goes into a great many things.

grub.name: a dead grub
grub.description: A pale, soft larva, limp and finger-length. Cheap food in a pinch, and the surest bait for what swims in the deep pools.

consumable.flavour: You swallow the raw grub down whole, trying not to taste it. Cold and soft, it is barely a mouthful — but it is something.

cave-fish.name: a blind cave-fish
cave-fish.description: A pale, eyeless fish hauled dripping from the black water, no longer than a hand. The flesh is cold and faintly translucent. Edible raw at a pinch, and far better over a fire — a staple catch for any camp that learns the trick of the line.

consumable.flavour: You eat the raw cave-fish in cold, soft mouthfuls. It is bland and faintly bitter, but it fills the hollow under your ribs.

roasted-grubs.name: a handful of roasted grubs
roasted-grubs.description: Fat grubs roasted on the hearthstones until they crisp and split. They pop between the teeth — poor man's fare, but hot and honest, and they sit far better cooked than raw.

consumable.flavour: You crunch through the roasted grubs. Greasy and warm, they take the worst of the edge off your hunger.

bug-meat.name: a cut of bug-meat
bug-meat.description: A pale, rubbery slab of muscle prised from under a grazer's shell, faintly sweet and slick with juice. It sits ill raw — but meat is meat this side of the dark, and the broth-pots of the Rim are not fussy.

consumable.flavour: You gnaw down the raw bug-meat. It is tough and cold and graceless going down, but it quiets the hollow in your gut.

rat-meat.name: a cut of rat-meat
rat-meat.description: A stringy, dark haunch off a giant rat. Gamey and tough raw — but meat is meat this near the dark, and cooked down in a broth it eats honest enough. Maeve buys it without asking where it came from.

consumable.flavour: You gnaw the raw rat-meat down. It is tough and gamey and cold, but it takes the worst edge off your hunger.

cooked-skewer.name: a bug-meat skewer
cooked-skewer.description: Cubes of bug-meat rolled in rendered tallow and charred over the inn's hearth, slid steaming off an iron spit. The fat crisps the outside and renders the rubber out of it; it eats hot, savoury, and almost respectable.

consumable.flavour: You eat the skewer hot off the spit. The seared, fat-crisped meat is rich and filling, and strength flows back into your limbs.

deep-stew.name: a bowl of deep stew
deep-stew.description: A thick, dark stew of bug-meat and palecap browned in tallow and simmered long in small beer until the whole bowl turns rich and heady. It is the closest thing to a feast a delver gets this side of the rim — it sits heavy and warm, mending flesh and steadying the mind both.

consumable.flavour: You eat the deep stew slowly, savouring it. Rich and warming, it fills you to the back teeth and a settled strength flows back into your limbs and your mind alike.

beer.name: a mug of beer
beer.description: A dented tin mug of the Rim's own small beer — thin, cloudy, and faintly bitter with cave-hops, brewed in the cellar under the Lantern's Rest. It will not get a delver drunk, but it steadies the nerves and washes the rock-dust from the throat.

consumable.flavour: You drink the beer down. It is thin and bitter, but a little of the day's weight lifts with it.

venom-gland.name: a venom gland
venom-gland.description: A taut, amber sac cut from a cave centipede's head, heavy with its festering bite. Handled carefully, it is an alchemist's prize; handled poorly, a mistake.

salamander-tail.name: a salamander tail
salamander-tail.description: The shed tail of a pale salamander, still faintly twitching — the creature grows another. Alchemists prize that knack, distilling it into salves that knit flesh.

scroll-summon-wisp.name: a Scroll of Summon Wisp
scroll-summon-wisp.description: Heavy vellum inked with a coiling binding-glyph that seems to tug faintly at the air around it. Study it to learn the Summon Wisp weave — a conjured mote of captive light that fights at your side — and the scroll crumbles to ash in the learning.

iron-sword.name: an iron sword
iron-sword.description: A straight, single-edged blade of abyssal iron, plain at the hilt and honest in the hand. No finer than a smith's first hundred, but it bites as well as bought steel and a delver can forge their own.

iron-mace.name: an iron mace
iron-mace.description: A blunt iron head on a short haft — no edge to dull, just weight, swung hard. It rewards a strong arm, cracking through shell and bone that turn a blade aside.

chitin-helm.name: a chitin helm
chitin-helm.description: A cap of curved stonebug scutes lashed over a leather lining, light for the cover it gives. It turns a blow as well as beaten iron and rides easier — but the shell closes close around the ears, and a delver hears the dark a little less keenly for it.

chitin-cuirass.name: a chitin cuirass
chitin-cuirass.description: Overlapping plates of grey stonebug shell, boiled hard and stitched onto a hide backing so they ride the body like a second carapace. It soaks up punishment a leather jerkin never could, lending a delver the grazer's own stubborn endurance — at the cost of some of their ear for the dark.

heavy-chitin-plate.name: a suit of heavy chitin plate
heavy-chitin-plate.description: A full harness of the thickest stonebug scutes, layered double and banded with iron until it stands near as stiff as the creature it came from. Almost nothing gets through it, and it carries the weight of a small wall — a delver inside it shrugs off blows that would fell them, but lumbers half-deaf and half-blind to the dark closing in.

dense-chitin-cuirass.name: a dense chitin cuirass
dense-chitin-cuirass.description: A cuirass beaten from slabs of dense, mineral-banded chitin — the near-black, ceramic-hard plate of the deep's oldest armoured things — fitted to Mallki's pattern and bound to an iron frame. It is the heaviest thing a delver can wear and turns aside very nearly everything, but it sits like a second skeleton of stone: a body inside it lumbers, slow to move and slower to strike, and the dark has long to close before a swing comes round.

schematic-dense-chitin-cuirass.name: a dense chitin method
schematic-dense-chitin-cuirass.description: A strip of cured gut-skin marked in Mallki's careful hand — the layering of the dense black plate, the working of the iron frame, the fitting of a harness heavy enough to stop a deep-thing's charge. No words, only the sequence of steps. Study it to fix the method in memory.

chitin-maul.name: a chitin maul
chitin-maul.description: A great two-fisted head of fused stonebug plate, banded to a stout haft. It is heavier than it looks and slow off the mark, but a delver who puts their back into it brings the whole weight of the deep's own armour down on a single point.

barbed-flail.name: a barbed flail
barbed-flail.description: A knot of thornbug spikes bound to a swinging head on a short chain — it does not so much strike as tear, leaving ragged, weeping wounds that will not close. Quick and light, it asks little strength; what it asks for is time, and a foe with nowhere to bleed out.

spiked-chitin-armor.name: spiked chitin armour
spiked-chitin-armor.description: A stonebug-shell cuirass studded all over with thornbug spikes, points turned outward like a cornered pillbug's. It guards a delver no worse than plain chitin — and makes the dark pay for laying hands on them, every blow drawing its own blood back on the barbs.

kingshell-cuirass.name: a kingshell cuirass
kingshell-cuirass.description: A cuirass shaped from the Pale King's own carapace, worked to Mallki's patient method — the glimmer-veined shell pared thin and curved close to the body over a frame of fine drawn silver, then woven through with a whole ground crystal so the cold light runs all through it. It is feather-light against the heft of iron or stacked chitin, no slab of plate but a glimmer-enhanced shell, and it carries the deep art with it: hostile weaves slide off the crust, and a blow that lands feeds a flicker of its spent light back into the wearer. Umbral glimmer-craft, and there can only ever be the one.

schematic-kingshell-cuirass.name: a kingshell method
schematic-kingshell-cuirass.description: A strip of cured gut-skin marked in Mallki's careful hand — the paring of the great shell, the grinding of the crystal, the working of the cold light all through the plate. No words, only the sequence of steps, drawn for a hand that will likely only ever do this once. Study it to fix the method in memory.

book-of-basic-smithing.name: a book of basic smithing
book-of-basic-smithing.description: A thin, soot-thumbed primer in Tobin's cramped hand — the honest fundamentals of the forge, ore to edge, with diagrams of a plain sword, a mace, a cuirass and a helm any delver could beat out with iron and patience. Study it to fix the lot in memory at once; the binding gives up its pages as you take in the last.

book-of-chitin-craft.name: a book of chitin craft
book-of-chitin-craft.description: A heavier, hard-won treatise — half Tobin's notes, half borrowed from older hands — on working the deep's own armour: how to boil and band stonebug plate into gear that turns a blade, and how to set a thornbug's barbs so they bite back. Rare knowledge, and priced like it. Study it to learn the whole tanky line at once.

book-of-glimmersteel.name: the Book of Glimmersteel
book-of-glimmersteel.description: A slim, careful volume bound in oiled vellum — Tobin's collected glimmer-work, set down at last in a fairer hand than his own soot-smudged diagrams. The folding and quenching of a glimmersteel blade, the casting of a great warhammer's head, the paring and curving of a cuirass, the doming of a helm, each drawn so the captive light beds into the metal instead of guttering out. He parts with it dearly, and only once you've the bar-work to follow it. Study it to fix the whole gear-line in memory at once.

schematic-gloom-silk.name: a gloom-silk method
schematic-gloom-silk.description: A narrow strip of cured gut-skin, marked with Mallki's careful hand — small diagrams of the moss-curtain, the drying rack, the drawing of the fibres. No words, only the sequence of steps. Study it to fix the method in memory.

weeping-chasm-moss.name: a clump of weeping chasm-moss
weeping-chasm-moss.description: A soft mass of bioluminescent moss, its long micro-fibrous tendrils still damp from the river air. It glows faintly and coldly — blue-white, like something that never learned there was a sun. Left to dry, the fibres can be drawn out and spun into something finer.

gloom-silk.name: a spool of gloom-silk
gloom-silk.description: Fine thread drawn from dried weeping chasm-moss fibres and processed at the alchemist's bench. Faintly luminous, cool to the touch, and stronger than it has any right to look. The stuff of deep-made cloth.

shadow-heart.name: a shadow-heart
shadow-heart.description: A knot of living dark, cut cold from the Starving Dark where its centre coiled thickest — no bigger than a fist, and heavier than a thing that size has any right to be. It drinks the light off your hands as you hold it, sheds a chill that has nothing to do with the deep, and never quite sits still in the palm. The Umbrals' glimmer-craft can bind such a heart into worked steel, if a delver knows the trick of it — and Mallki, of all the deep-folk, is the one to ask.

book-of-shadow-binding.name: the Book of Shadow-Binding
book-of-shadow-binding.description: A slim folio of dark, supple hide, worked in Mallki's careful hand and the rounded glyphs of the deep-folk — a glimmer-craft the surface has no name for: the binding of a captured shadow's heart into worked steel, so the hunger of the dark is carried in an edge. Study it to take the working into memory; the deep-folk's secrets are not given twice.

hungering-dagger.name: a hungering dagger
hungering-dagger.description: A short, dark blade with a shadow-heart bound cold into the steel of it, so the metal never quite warms and the edge seems to drink the light along its length. It carries the Starving Dark's hunger: every cut it lands draws a little of the life out of the wounded and feeds it back to the hand on the hilt. Quick, and never quite sated.
